QF.id: SEBI Guidelines for AML Standards, CFT and PF with KYC Norms_23

question.text: Under SEBI AML/CFT guidelines, how long must records relating to ongoing investigations or suspicious transaction reporting be retained?

answer.text: Until it is confirmed that the case has been closed
For a minimum period of 5 years from the date of the suspicious transaction report
For a minimum period of 7 years from the date of filing the report with FIU-IND
For a minimum period of 10 years from the date of the last transaction in the account

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** While the general record retention period under AML/CFT guidelines is 5 years, there is a critical exception for records relating to ongoing investigations or transactions that have been the subject of a suspicious transaction reporting. In such cases, records must be retained until it is confirmed that the case has been closed. This is an open-ended retention requirement tied to case closure, not a fixed time period.
**The Trap:** Candidates who memorise only the standard 5-year retention rule will select that option. The question specifically asks about records related to ongoing investigations or STR cases, which triggers the exception. The 7-year and 10-year options are fabricated fixed periods designed to tempt candidates who assume a longer fixed period applies.
**Cross-Exam Context:** This exception ensures that evidence and audit trails remain available for the entire duration of enforcement proceedings, which can extend well beyond the standard retention period.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The standard 5-year record retention period does not apply when records relate to ongoing investigations or suspicious transaction reports - such records must be kept until case closure is confirmed, regardless of how long that takes.
